name: Hand

unions.name: Empty
unions.comment:
A word used in the [`Hand`](#Hand) type:

    top : Hand (Stacked element) Never Empty -> element

    when :
        Hand ... possiblyOrNever_ Empty
        -> Hand ... Possibly Empty

unions.name: Hand
unions.comment:
Like `Maybe`, but able to know at type-level whether [`Empty`](#Empty) is possible.

    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled, fill)

    [ filled 1, filled 7 ]
        --: List (Hand number_ never_ Empty)
        |> List.map fill
    --> [ 1, 7 ]

[`Hand.Empty`](#Empty) alone probably won't be that useful,
but it can make data structures type-safely non-emptiable:

    import Hand exposing (fillMap)

    top : Hand (Stacked element) Never Empty -> element

    fillMap Dict.NonEmpty.head
    --: Hand (NonEmptyDict comparable v) possiblyOrNever Empty
    --: -> Hand ( comparable, v ) possiblyOrNever Empty

Go take a look at all the data structures in this package.

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Insert joke about life here.

    Hand.empty
        |> Hand.fillMap (\x -> x / 0)
    --> Hand.empty
values.type: Hand.Hand filling_ Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: emptyAdapt
values.comment:
Change the `possiblyOrNever` type.


#### Returning a type declaration value or argument that is `Empty Possibly`

An `Empty possiblyOrNever` can't be used as `Empty Possibly`

    import Hand exposing (Empty)
    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Stack exposing (Stacked)

    type alias Log =
        Empty Possibly (Stacked String)

    fromStack : Hand (Stacked String) possiblyOrNever_ Empty -> Log
    fromStack stackFilled =
        stackFilled
            --: `possiblyOrNever_` but we need `Possibly`
            |> Hand.emptyAdapt (always Possible)


#### An argument or a type declaration value is `Never Empty`

The `Never Empty` can't be unified with `Possibly Empty` or a type variable

    import Hand exposing (Empty)
    import Stack exposing (Stacked)

    theShorter :
        Hand (Stacked element) Never Empty
        -> Hand (Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Empty
        -> Hand (Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Empty
    theShorter aStack bStack =
        if Stack.length bStack > Stack.length aStack then
            bStack

        else
            aStack
                --: `Never` but we need `possiblyOrNever`
                |> Hand.emptyAdapt never

makes both branches return `possiblyOrNever`.
values.type: (possiblyOrNever -> adaptedPossiblyOrNever) -> Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand fill adaptedPossiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: fill
values.comment:
Safely extract the [`filled`](#filled) content from a `Hand fill Never Empty`.

    import Hand exposing (Empty, filled)

    filled (filled (filled "Bami"))
        |> Hand.fill
        |> Hand.fill
        |> Hand.fill
    --> "Bami"

    first : Empty ( first, others_ ) Never Empty -> first
    first =
        Hand.fill >> Tuple.first
values.type: Hand.Hand fill Basics.Never Hand.Empty -> fill

values.name: fillAnd
values.comment:
If the incoming food and the given argument are
[`filled`](#filled), give a [`filled`](#filled) tuple of both [`fill`](#fill)s back.

If any is [`empty`](#empty), give a [`Hand.empty`](#empty) back.

[`fillAnd`](#fillAnd) comes in handy when **multiple arguments** need to be [`filled`](#filled):

    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap, fillAnd)

    filled 3
        |> fillAnd (filled 4)
        |> fillAnd (filled 5)
        |> fillMap (\( ( a0, a1 ), a2 ) -> a0^a1 - a2^2)
    --> filled 56
values.type: Hand.Hand anotherFill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand ( fill, anotherFill ) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: fillElseOnEmpty
values.comment:
Lazily use a fallback value if the [`Hand`](#Hand) is [`empty`](#empty).

    import Possibly exposing (Possibly(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand(..), fillElseOnEmpty)
    import Dict

    Dict.empty
        |> Dict.get "Hannah"
        |> Hand.fromMaybe
        |> fillElseOnEmpty (\_ -> "unknown")
    --> "unknown"

    fill =
        fillElseOnEmpty never

    fatten =
        fillElseOnEmpty Empty
values.type: (possiblyOrNever -> fill) -> Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> fill

values.name: fillMap
values.comment:
If the [`Hand`](#Hand) is [`filled`](#filled), change it based on its current [`fill`](#fill):

    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap)

    filled -3 |> fillMap abs
    --> filled 3

    Hand.empty |> fillMap abs
    --> Hand.empty
values.type: (fill -> fillMapped) -> Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand fillMapped possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: fillMapFlat
values.comment:
Chain together operations that may return [`empty`](#empty).
It's like calling [`fillMap`](#fillMap)`|>`[`flatten`](#flatten):

If the argument [`Hand.Empty`](#Empty) `Never` [`Empty`](Hand#Empty),
a given function takes its [`fill`](#fill)
and returns a new possibly [`empty`](#empty) value.

Some call it
[`andThen`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/core/latest/Maybe#andThen)
or [`flatMap`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/ccapndave/elm-flat-map/1.2.0/Maybe-FlatMap#flatMap).

    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, fillMapFlat)

    emptiableString
        |> fillMapFlat parse
        |> fillMapFlat extraValidation

    parse : ( Char, String ) -> Hand Parsed Possibly Empty
    extraValidation : Parsed -> Hand Parsed Possibly Empty

For any number of arguments:
[`fillAnd`](#fillAnd)`... |> ... |>`[`fillMapFlat`](#fillMapFlat):

    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMapFlat, fillAnd)

    (filled 3)
        |> fillAnd (filled 4)
        |> fillAnd (filled 5)
        |> fillMapFlat
            (\( ( a, b ), c ) -> filled ( a, b, c ))
    --> filled ( 3, 4, 5 )
values.type: (fill -> Hand.Hand fillIfBothFilled possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty) -> Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand fillIfBothFilled possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: filled
values.comment:
A [`Hand.Empty`](#Empty) that certainly exists, allowing type-safe extraction.

    import Hand exposing (filled, fill)

    filled "Bami" |> fill
    --> "Bami"
values.type: fill -> Hand.Hand fill never_ Hand.Empty

values.name: flatten
values.comment:
In a nestable [`Hand`](#Hand):
Only keep it [`filled`](#filled) if the inner [`Hand`](#Hand) is [`filled`](#filled).

Some call it [`join`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-community/maybe-extra/latest/Maybe-Extra#join).

    import Hand exposing (filled)

    filled (filled 1) |> Hand.flatten
    --> filled 1

    filled Hand.empty |> Hand.flatten
    --> Hand.empty

    Hand.empty |> Hand.flatten
    --> Hand.empty
values.type: Hand.Hand (Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: fromMaybe
values.comment:
Convert a `Maybe` to a [`Hand`](#Hand)`Possibly`[`Hand`](#Empty).

To _create_ new [`Hand`](#Hand)s, use [`filled`](#filled) and [`empty`](#empty) instead!
values.type: Maybe.Maybe value -> Hand.Hand value Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment:
Convert to a `Maybe`.

Don't try to use this prematurely.
Keeping type information as long as possible is always a win.
values.type: Hand.Hand fill possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty -> Maybe.Maybe fill

name: Scroll

unions.name: FocusGap
unions.comment:
A word in every [`Scroll`](#Scroll) type:

  - `🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇`: `Scroll ... Never FocusGap`

  - `🍍 🍓 <?> 🍉 🍇`: `Scroll ...` [`Possibly`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-allowable-state/latest/Possibly) `FocusGap`

    `<?>` means both are possible:

      - `🍍 🍓 <> 🍉 🍇`: a gap between items ... Heh.
      - `🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇`

unions.name: Location
unions.comment:
Position in a [`Scroll`](#Scroll) relative to its focus.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled, fillMap, fillMapFlat)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.to (Scroll.AtSide Up 2)
        |> fillMap Scroll.focusItem
    --> filled 3
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty

unions.name: Scroll
unions.comment:
Items rolled up on both sides of a focus
→ good fit for dynamic choice selection: tabs, playlist, ...

`Scroll` can even focus a gap `Down` and `Up` every item:

  - `🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇`: `Scroll ... Never FocusGap`

  - `🍍 🍓 <?> 🍉 🍇`: `Scroll ...` [`Possibly`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-allowable-state/latest/Possibly) `FocusGap`

    `<?>` means both are possible:

      - `🍍 🍓 <> 🍉 🍇`: a gap between items ... Heh.
      - `🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇`


#### in arguments

    empty : Scroll item_ Possibly FocusGap


#### in types

    type alias Model =
        RecordWithoutConstructorFunction
            { choice : Scroll Option Never FocusGap
            }

where [`RecordWithoutConstructorFunction`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-no-record-type-alias-constructor-function/latest/)
stops the compiler from creating a constructor function for `Model`.

values.name: empty
values.comment:
An empty `Scroll` on a gap
with nothing before and after it.
It's the loneliest of all [`Scroll`](#Scroll)s.

```monospace
<>
```

    import Hand

    Scroll.empty |> Scroll.toStack
    --> Hand.empty
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item_ Possibly.Possibly Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: focus
values.comment:
The focused item or gap.

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊
🍍 🍓 <> 🍉 🍇  ->  _
```

    import Hand exposing (filled, fill)

    Scroll.empty |> Scroll.focus
    --> Hand.empty

    Scroll.only "hi there" |> Scroll.focus |> fill
    --> "hi there"

[`focusItem`](#focusItem) is short for `focus |> fill`.
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand item possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: focusAlter
values.comment:
Alter the focus – [item or gap](Hand) – based on its current value.


#### `Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> 🍊 |> filled)`

```monospace
🍊  ->  🍓 <?> 🍉  ->  🍓 <🍊> 🍉
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap)
    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopLay)

    Scroll.empty
            -- <>
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, onTopLay "🍓" )
            -- "🍓" <>
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, onTopLay "🍉" )
            -- "🍓" <> "🍉"
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> "🍊" |> filled)
            -- "🍓" <"🍊"> "🍉"
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "🍓" [ "🍊", "🍉" ]


#### `Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> Hand.empty)`

```monospace
🍓 <?> 🍉  ->  🍓 <> 🍉
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "hello"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "scrollable" [ "world" ] )
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
        |> fillMap (Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> Hand.empty))
        |> fillMap Scroll.toList
    --> filled [ "hello", "world" ]


#### `Scroll.focusAlter (?🍒 -> ?🍊)`

```monospace
(?🍒 -> ?🍊)  ->  🍓 <?🍒> 🍉  ->  🍓 <?🍊> 🍉
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap)
    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopLay)

    Scroll.empty
            -- <>
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, onTopLay "🍓" )
            -- "🍓" <>
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, onTopLay "🍉" )
            -- "🍓" <> "🍉"
        |> Scroll.focusAlter
            (\_ -> filled "🍊")
            -- "🍓" <"🍊"> "🍉"
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "🍓" [ "🍊", "🍉" ]

    Scroll.only "first"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> Stack.only "zeroth" )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "second" [ "third" ] )
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (fillMap String.toUpper)
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "zeroth" [ "FIRST", "second", "third" ]
values.type: (Hand.Hand item possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand item possiblyOrNeverAltered Hand.Empty) -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNeverAltered Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: focusDrag
values.comment:
Try to move the focus to the nearest item [`Down|Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

`Down`

```monospace
🍊 🍉 <🍓> 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍓> 🍉 🍇
🍊 🍉 <> 🍇  ->  🍊 <> 🍉 🍇
```

`Up`

```monospace
🍊 <🍓> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊 🍉 <🍓> 🍇
🍊 <> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊 🍉 <> 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, fillMapFlat)
    import Scroll exposing (Scroll, FocusGap)

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.focusDrag Down
        |> fillMapFlat Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 3 [ 2, 0, 1 ]
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.focusDrag Up
        |> fillMapFlat Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 1 [ 0, 2, 3 ]
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty

If there is no nearest item, the result is [`empty`](Hand#empty).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.focusDrag Down
    --> Hand.empty

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.focusDrag Up
    --> Hand.empty
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand (Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: focusGapAdapt
values.comment:
Change the `possiblyOrNever` type

  - A `Scroll ... possiblyOrNever FocusGap`
    can't be used as a `Scroll ... Possibly`?

        import Possibly exposing (Possibly(..))

        Scroll.focusGapAdapt (always Possible)

  - A `Scroll ... Never FocusGap`
    can't be unified with `Scroll ... Possibly` or `possiblyOrNever FocusGap`?

        Scroll.focusGapAdapt never

Please read more at [`Hand.emptyAdapt`](Hand#emptyAdapt).
values.type: (possiblyOrNever -> adaptedPossiblyOrNever) -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item adaptedPossiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: focusItem
values.comment:
The focused item.

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊
```

    import Stack exposing (topDown)
    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    Scroll.only "hi there" |> Scroll.focusItem
    --> "hi there"

    Scroll.only 1
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 3, 4 ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.focusItem
    --> 4
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item Basics.Never Scroll.FocusGap -> item

values.name: focusItemTry
values.comment:
[`Hand.empty`](Hand#empty) if the current focussed thing is a gap,
[`Hand.filled`](Hand#filled) if it's an item.

    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)
    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    Scroll.only 3
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 1 ] )
        |> Scroll.toGap Up
        |> Scroll.focusItemTry
    --> Hand.empty
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand (Scroll.Scroll item never_ Scroll.FocusGap) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: focusSidesMap
values.comment:
Change the [`focus`](#focus),
the [`side`](#side)s `Down` and `Up`
using different functions.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "first"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Stack.only "second" )
        |> Scroll.toGap Up
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> filled "one-and-a-halfth")
        |> Scroll.focusSidesMap
            { side =
                \side ->
                    Stack.map
                        (\_ item ->
                            String.concat
                                [ side |> sideToString, ": ", item ]
                        )
            , focus =
                fillMap (\item -> "focused item: " ++ item)
            }
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --→
    topDown
        "before: first"
        [ "focused item: one-and-a-halfth"
        , "after: second"
        ]

    sideToString =
        \side ->
            case side of
                Down ->
                    "before"

                Up ->
                    "after"

[`map`](#map) transforms every item.
values.type: { focus : Hand.Hand item possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand mappedItem possiblyOrNeverMapped Hand.Empty, side : Linear.DirectionLinear -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked item) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked mappedItem) possiblyOrNeverMappedBefore_ Hand.Empty } -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll mappedItem possiblyOrNeverMapped Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: fold
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, item -> item -> item ) -> Scroll.Scroll item Basics.Never Scroll.FocusGap -> item

values.name: foldFrom
values.comment:
Reduce in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only 'e'
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 'v' [ 'i', 'l' ] )
        |> Scroll.foldFrom ( "", Down, String.cons )
    --> "live"

    Scroll.only 'e'
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 'v' [ 'i', 'l' ] )
        |> Scroll.foldFrom ( "", Up, String.cons )
    --> "evil"
values.type: ( accumulationValue, Linear.DirectionLinear, item -> accumulationValue -> accumulationValue ) -> Scroll.Scroll item Basics.Never Scroll.FocusGap -> accumulationValue

values.name: length
values.comment:
Counting all contained items.

    import Stack exposing (topDown)
    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown -1 [ -2 ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.length
    --> 6

    Scroll.empty
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown -1 [ -2 ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.length
    --> 5
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item_ possiblyOrNever_ Scroll.FocusGap -> Basics.Int

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every item based on its current value.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "first"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> Stack.only "zeroth" )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "second" [ "third" ] )
        |> Scroll.map (\_ -> String.toUpper)
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "ZEROTH" [ "FIRST", "SECOND", "THIRD" ]

[`focusSidesMap`](#focusSidesMap) allows changing the individual parts separately.
values.type: (Scroll.Location -> item -> mappedItem) -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll mappedItem possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: mirror
values.comment:
Swap the [stack](Stack) on the [`side Down`](#side) the [`focus`](#focus)
with the [stack](Stack) on the [`side Up`](#side).

```monospace
🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇  <->  🍇 🍉 <🍊> 🍓
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only 1
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 3, 4 ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 4 [ 3, 2 ] )

In contrast to `List` or [stack](Stack), this can be done in `O(1)` time.
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: nearest
values.comment:
The [`Location`](#Location) directly [`Down`|`Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) the focus.

    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled, fillMap)
    import Stack exposing (onTopLay, topDown)
    import Scroll exposing (Scroll, FocusGap)
    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    Scroll.only "hello"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "scrollable" [ "world" ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.to (Down |> Scroll.nearest)
        |> fillMap Scroll.focusItem
    --> filled "scrollable"
    --: Hand (Scroll String Never FocusGap) Possibly Empty

    Scroll.empty
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown "world" [ "scrollable" ] )
        |> Scroll.to (Down |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> Scroll.only "world"
    -->     |> Scroll.sideAlter
    -->         ( Down, \_ -> Stack.only "scrollable" )
    -->     |> filled
    --: Hand (Scroll String Never FocusGap) Possibly Empty

    Scroll.empty
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, onTopLay "foo" )
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> filled (Scroll.only "foo")
    --: Hand (Scroll String Never FocusGap) Possibly

    nearest =
        \side ->
            Scroll.AtSide side 0
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> Scroll.Location

values.name: only
values.comment:
A `Scroll` with a single focussed item in it,
nothing `Down` and `Up` it.

```monospace
🍊  ->  <🍊>
```

    import Stack

    Scroll.only "wat" |> Scroll.focusItem
    --> "wat"

    Scroll.only "wat" |> Scroll.toStack
    --> Stack.only "wat"
values.type: element -> Scroll.Scroll element never_ Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: side
values.comment:
The [`Stack`](Stack) to one [side](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) of the focus.

`Down`

```monospace
🍍←🍓) <🍊> 🍉 🍇
```

`Up`

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> (🍉→🍇
```

    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, fillMapFlat)
    import Stack exposing (Stacked, topDown)
    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
        |> fillMapFlat (Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest))
        |> fillMapFlat (Scroll.side Down)
    --> topDown 1 [ 0 ]
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
        |> fillMapFlat (Scroll.side Up)
    --> topDown 2 [ 3 ]
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever_ Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked item) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: sideAlter
values.comment:
Look [`Down|Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) the [`focus`](#focus) and operate directly an the [`Stack`](Stack) you see.


#### `sideAlter ( DirectionLinear, \_ -> 🍒🍋 )`

`Down`

```monospace
🍓 <🍊> 🍉
      ↓
🍋 🍒 <🍊> 🍉
```

`Up`

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉
      ↓
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍒 🍋
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "selectoo"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown "earlee" [ "agua", "enutai" ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "orangloo" [ "iquipy", "oice" ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Hand.empty )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "enutai" [ "agua", "earlee", "selectoo" ]


#### `sideAlter ( DirectionLinear, Stack.map ... )`

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "second"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown "first" [ "zeroth" ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, Stack.map (\_ -> String.toUpper) )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "ZEROTH" [ "FIRST", "second" ]

    Scroll.only "zeroth"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "first" [ "second" ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, Stack.map (\_ -> String.toUpper) )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "zeroth" [ "FIRST", "SECOND" ]

Look to one [side](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) from the focus
and slide items in directly at the nearest location.


#### `sideAlter ( DirectionLinear, Stack.onTopGlue/onTopStack 🍒🍋 )`

`Down`

```monospace
      🍒🍋
🍍 🍓 \↓/ <🍊> 🍉
```

`Up`

```monospace
        🍒🍋
🍓 <🍊> \↓/ 🍉 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, Stack.onTopGlue [ -4, -5 ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, Stack.onTopStack (topDown -1 [ -2, -3 ]) )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown -5 [ -4, -3, -2, -1, 0 ]

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, Stack.onTopGlue [ 4, 5 ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, Stack.onTopStack (topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ]) )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ]


#### `Scroll.sideAlter ( DirectionLinear, \side -> 🍒🍋 |> onTopStack side )`

`Down`

```monospace
🍋🍒
 \↓ 🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉
```

`Up`

```monospace
              🍒🍋
🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇 ↓/
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopStack)

    Scroll.only 1
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \after -> topDown 2 [ 3, 4 ] |> onTopStack after )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \before -> topDown 7 [ 6, 5 ] |> onTopStack before )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 5 [ 6, 7, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

    Scroll.only 123
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \after -> Stack.only 456 |> onTopStack after )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \after -> topDown 789 [ 0 ] |> onTopStack after )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 123 [ 456, 789, 0 ]


#### `sideAlter ( DirectionLinear,`[`Stack.onTopLay`](Stack#onTopLay) `... )`

`Down`

```monospace
      🍒
🍍 🍓 ↓ <🍊> 🍉
```


#### `Up`

```monospace
        🍒
🍓 <🍊> ↓ 🍉 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopLay)

    Scroll.only 123
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, onTopLay 456 )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 456 [ 123 ]

    Scroll.only 123
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Stack.only 789 )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, onTopLay 456 )
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 123 [ 456, 789 ]
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked item) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked item) possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty ) -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: to
values.comment:
Try to move the [`focus`](#focus) to the item at a given [`Scroll.Location`](#Location).


#### `Scroll.to (Down |> Scroll.nearest)`

```monospace
🍊 <🍉> 🍇  ->  <🍊> 🍉 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled, fillMap)
    import Scroll exposing (Scroll, FocusGap)

    Scroll.empty |> Scroll.to (Down |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> Hand.empty

    Scroll.only "hello"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown "scrollable" [ "world" ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.to (Down |> Scroll.nearest)
        |> fillMap Scroll.focusItem
    --> filled "scrollable"
    --: Hand (Scroll String Never FocusGap) Possibly Empty

This also works from within gaps:

```monospace
🍊 🍉 <> 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍉> 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled)
    import Stack exposing (onTopLay)
    import Scroll exposing (Scroll, FocusGap)

    Scroll.empty
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, onTopLay "foo" )
        |> Scroll.to (Down |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> filled (Scroll.only "foo")
    --: Hand (Scroll String Never FocusGap) Possibly Empty


#### `Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)`

```monospace
<🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍉> 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled, fillMap)
    import Scroll exposing (Scroll, FocusGap)

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
        |> fillMap Scroll.focusItem
    --> filled 1
    --: Hand number_ Possibly Empty

This also works from within gaps:

```monospace
🍊 <> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍊 <🍉> 🍇
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty, filled)
    import Stack exposing (onTopLay)
    import Scroll exposing (Scroll, FocusGap)

    Scroll.empty
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Stack.only "foo" )
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> filled (Scroll.only "foo")
    --: Hand (Scroll String Never FocusGap) Possibly

If there is no next item, the result is [`empty`](Hand#empty).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.empty |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> Hand.empty

    Scroll.only 0
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.to (Up |> Scroll.nearest)
    --> Hand.empty


#### `Scroll.to Location`

    import Element as Ui
    import Element.Input as UIn
    import Hand exposing (fillMap)
    import RecordWithoutConstructorFunction exposing (RecordWithoutConstructorFunction)
    import Scroll exposing (FocusGap, Scroll)
    import Stack

    type alias Model =
        RecordWithoutConstructorFunction
            { numbers : Scroll Int Never FocusGap
            }

    type Event
        = NumberClicked Scroll.Location

    update : Event -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Event )
    update event model =
        case event of
            NumberClicked location ->
                ( { model
                    | numbers =
                        model.numbers
                            |> Scroll.to location
                            |> Hand.fillElseOnEmpty (\_ -> model.numbers)
                            |> Scroll.focusAlter (fillMap (\n -> n + 1))
                  }
                , Cmd.none
                )

    interface : Model -> Ui.Element Event
    interface =
        \{ numbers } ->
            numbers
                |> Scroll.map numberInterface
                |> Scroll.toList
                |> Ui.column []

    numberInterface :
        Scroll.Location
        -> Int
        -> Ui.Element Event
    numberInterface location =
        \number ->
            UIn.button []
                { onPress = NumberClicked location |> Just
                , label =
                    number
                        |> String.fromInt
                        |> Ui.text
                }

The same _functionality_ is often provided as `duplicate : Scroll item ... -> Scroll (Scroll item) ...`

  - [turboMaCk/non-empty-list-alias: `List.NonEmpty.Zipper.duplicate`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/turboMaCk/non-empty-list-alias/latest/List-NonEmpty-Zipper#duplicate)
  - [miyamoen/select-list: `SelectList.selectedMap`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/miyamoen/select-list/latest/SelectList#selectedMap)
  - [jjant/elm-comonad-zipper: `Zipper.duplicate`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/jjant/elm-comonad-zipper/latest/Zipper#duplicate)
  - [arowM/elm-reference: `Reference.List.unwrap`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/arowM/elm-reference/latest/Reference-List#unwrap)

Saving a [`Scroll`](#Scroll) with every item becomes expensive for long [`Scroll`](#Scroll)s, though!
values.type: Scroll.Location -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever_ Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand (Scroll.Scroll item never_ Scroll.FocusGap) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: toEnd
values.comment:
Focus the furthest item [`Down/Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) the focus.

`Down`

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉  ->  <🍍> 🍓 🍊 🍉
```

`Up`

```monospace
🍓 <🍊> 🍉 🍇  ->  🍓 🍊 🍉 <🍇>
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (Hand, Empty)
    import Stack exposing (Stacked, topDown)

    Scroll.only 1
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 3, 4 ] )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 4 [ 3, 2 ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Down
        |> Scroll.focusItem
    --> 2

    Scroll.only 1
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 3, 4 ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.focusItem
    --> 4

    Scroll.only 1
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 3, 4 ] )
        |> Scroll.toEnd Up
        |> Scroll.side Down
    --> topDown 3 [ 2, 1 ]
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: toEndGap
values.comment:
Focus the gap beyond the furthest item [`Down|Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).
Remember that gaps surround everything!

`Down`

```monospace
🍍 🍓 <🍊> 🍉  ->  <> 🍍 🍓 🍊 🍉
```

`Up`

```monospace
🍓 <🍊> 🍉  ->  🍓 🍊 🍉 <>
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only 1
            -- <1>
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 3 [ 4 ] )
            -- <1> 3 4
        |> Scroll.toGap Up
            -- 1 <> 3 4
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> filled 2)
            -- 1 <2> 3 4
        |> Scroll.toEndGap Down
            -- <> 1 2 3 4
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> filled 0)
            -- <0> 1 2 3 4
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 0 [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

    Scroll.only 1
            -- <1>
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ 3 ] )
            -- <1> 2 3
        |> Scroll.toEndGap Up
            -- 1 2 3 <>
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> filled 4)
            -- 1 2 3 <4>
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 1 [ 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever_ Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item Possibly.Possibly Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: toGap
values.comment:
Move the focus to the gap directly [`Down|Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).
Feel free to [plug](#focusAlter) that gap right up!


#### `Scroll.toGap Down`

```monospace
🍍 <🍊> 🍉  ->  🍍 <> 🍊 🍉
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "world"
        |> Scroll.toGap Down
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> "hello" |> filled)
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "hello" [ "world" ]


#### `Scroll.toGap Up`

```monospace
🍍 <🍊> 🍉  ->  🍍 🍊 <> 🍉
```

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.only "hello"
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Stack.only "world" )
        |> Scroll.toGap Up
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_ -> filled "scrollable")
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown "hello" [ "scrollable", "world" ]
values.type: Linear.DirectionLinear -> Scroll.Scroll item Basics.Never Scroll.FocusGap -> Scroll.Scroll item Possibly.Possibly Scroll.FocusGap

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Converts it to a `List`, rolled out to both ends:

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack

    Scroll.only 456
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> Stack.only 123 )
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Stack.only 789 )
        |> Scroll.toList
    --> [ 123, 456, 789 ]

Only use this if you need a list in the end.
Otherwise, use [`toStack`](#toStack) to preserve some information about its length.
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever_ Scroll.FocusGap -> List.List item

values.name: toStack
values.comment:
Roll out the `Scroll` to both ends into a [`Stack`](Stack):

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    Scroll.empty
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> Hand.empty

    Scroll.only 123
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> Stack.only 789 )
        |> Scroll.toGap Up
        |> Scroll.focusAlter (\_-> filled 456)
        |> Scroll.toStack
    --> topDown 123 [ 456, 789 ]

the type information gets carried over, so

    Never Scroll.FocusGap -> Stacked Never Empty
    Possibly Scroll.FocusGap -> Stacked Possibly Empty
values.type: Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked item) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: toWhere
values.comment:
Move the focus to the nearest item [`Down|Up`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) that matches a predicate.
If no such item was found return with [`Hand.empty`](Hand#empty).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Hand exposing (filled, fillMap)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)
    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))

    Scroll.only 4
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Down, \_ -> topDown 2 [ -1, 0, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.toWhere ( Down, \_ item -> item < 0 )
        |> Hand.fillMap Scroll.focusItem
    --> filled -1

    Scroll.only 4
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ -1, 0, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.toWhere ( Up, \_ item -> item < 0 )
        |> fillMap focusItem
    --> filled -1

    Scroll.only -4
        |> Scroll.sideAlter
            ( Up, \_ -> topDown 2 [ -1, 0, 3 ] )
        |> Scroll.toWhere ( Up, \_ item -> item < 0 )
        |> fillMap focusItem
    --> filled -4
values.type: ( Linear.DirectionLinear, { index : Basics.Int } -> item -> Basics.Bool ) -> Scroll.Scroll item possiblyOrNever_ Scroll.FocusGap -> Hand.Hand (Scroll.Scroll item never_ Scroll.FocusGap) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

name: Stack

unions.name: StackTopBelow
unions.comment:
[`Stacked`](#Stacked) with a different type for the top element:

    top : Hand (StackTopBelow top belowTopElement_) Never Empty -> top

it's the result of:

  - [`only`](#only)
  - [`topDown`](#topDown)
  - [`fromTopDown`](#fromTopDown)
  - [`onTopLay`](#onTopLay)
  - [`topMap`](#topMap)

aliases.name: Stacked
aliases.comment:
A non-empty representation of a stack. [`top`](#top), [`topRemove`](#topRemove), [`onTopLay`](#onTopLay) [`topMap`](#topMap) are `O(n)`


#### in arguments

[`Hand`](Hand) `(StackFilled ...) Never Empty` → `stack` is non-empty:

    top : Hand (Stacked element) Never -> element


#### in results

[`Hand`](Hand) `(StackFilled ...)`[`Possibly`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-allowable-state/latest/Possibly) `Empty` → stack could be empty

    fromList : List element -> Hand (Stacked element) Possibly Empty

We can treat it like any [`Hand`](Hand):

    import Hand exposing (Hand(..), Empty, filled, fillMap)
    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Stack exposing (Stacked, top, onTopLay)

    Hand.empty |> onTopLay "cherry" -- works

    toList : Empty possiblyOrNever_ (Stacked element) -> List element
    toList =
        \stack ->
            case stack of
                Filled (Stack.TopDown top_ down) ->
                    top_ :: down

                Empty _ ->
                    []

    [ "hi", "there" ] -- comes in as an argument
        |> Stack.fromList
        |> fillMap (filled >> top)
    --: Hand String Possibly Empty

Most operations also allow a different type for the top element → see [`StackTopBelow`](#StackTopBelow)

values.name: and
values.comment:
Combine its elements with elements of a given stack at the same location.
If one stack is longer, the extra elements are dropped.

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown "alice" [ "bob", "chuck" ]
        |> Stack.and (topDown 2 [ 5, 7, 8 ])
    --> topDown ( "alice", 2 ) [ ( "bob", 5 ), ( "chuck", 7 ) ]

    topDown 4 [ 5, 6 ]
        |> Stack.and (topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ])
        |> Stack.map (\_ ( n0, n1 ) -> n0 + n1)
    --> topDown 5 [ 7, 9 ]
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked anotherElement) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked ( element, anotherElement )) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: belowTopMap
values.comment:
Change every element below its [`top`](#top)
based on their `{ index }` in the whole stack and their current value.
Their type is allowed to change.

    import Stack exposing (topDown, belowTopMap)

    topDown 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> belowTopMap (\_ -> negate)
    --> topDown 1 [ -4, -9 ]
values.type: ({ index : Basics.Int } -> belowElement -> belowElementMapped) -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElement) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElementMapped) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: fills
values.comment:
Keep all [`filled`](Hand#filled) elements
and drop all [`empty`](Hand#empty) elements.

    import Hand exposing (filled)
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown Hand.empty [ Hand.empty ]
        |> Stack.fills
    --> Hand.empty

    topDown (filled 1) [ Hand.empty, filled 3 ]
        |> Stack.fills
    --> topDown 1 [ 3 ]

As you can see, if only the top is [`fill`](Hand#fill) a value, the result is non-empty.
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow (Hand.Hand topContent possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty) (Hand.Hand belowElementContent belowPossiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty)) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow topContent belowElementContent) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: flatten
values.comment:
Glue together a bunch of stacks.

    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown
        (topDown 0 [ 1 ])
        [ topDown 10 [ 11 ]
        , Hand.empty
        , topDown 20 [ 21, 22 ]
        ]
        |> Stack.flatten
    --> topDown 0 [ 1, 10, 11, 20, 21, 22 ]

For this to return a non-empty stack, there must be a non-empty [`top`](#top) stack.
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow (Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty) (Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) belowTopListsPossiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty)) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Fold from the [`top`](#top) as the initial accumulation value.

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown 234 [ 345, 543 ]
        |> Stack.fold max
    --> 543

`fold` doesn't take a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/)
as an argument because at the time of writing, the best implementation would include a [`Stack.reverse`](#reverse).
values.type: (belowElement -> top -> top) -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElement) Basics.Never Hand.Empty -> top

values.name: foldFrom
values.comment:
Reduce in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    import Linear exposing (DirectionLinear(..))
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown 'l' [ 'i', 'v', 'e' ]
        |> Stack.foldFrom ( "", Down, String.cons )
    --> "live"

    topDown 'l' [ 'i', 'v', 'e' ]
        |> Stack.foldFrom ( "", Up, String.cons )
    --> "evil"

Be aware:

  - `Down` = indexes decreasing, not: from the [`top`](#top) down
  - `Up` = indexes increasing, not: from the bottom up
values.type: ( accumulationValue, Linear.DirectionLinear, element -> accumulationValue -> accumulationValue ) -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty -> accumulationValue

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Convert a `List element` to a `Empty Possibly (Stacked element)`.
The `List`s `head` becomes [`top`](#top), its `tail` is shoved down below.

    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    [] |> Stack.fromList
    --> Hand.empty

    [ "hello", "emptiness" ] |> Stack.fromList
    --> topDown "hello" [ "emptiness" ]
    --: Empty Possibly (Stacked String)

When constructing from known elements, always prefer

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown "hello" [ "emptiness" ]
values.type: List.List element -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: fromString
values.comment:
Convert a `String` to a `Hand (Stacked Char) Possibly Empty`.
The `String`s head becomes [`top`](#top), its tail is shoved down below.

    import Possibly exposing (Possibly)
    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    "" |> Stack.fromString
    --> Hand.empty

    "hello" |> Stack.fromString
    --> topDown 'h' [ 'e', 'l', 'l', 'o' ]
    --: Hand (Stacked Char) Possibly Empty

When constructing from known elements, always prefer

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    onTopLay 'h' ("ello" |> Stack.fromString)
values.type: String.String -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked Char.Char) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty

values.name: fromTopDown
values.comment:
Convert from a tuple `( top, List belowElement )`.

Use [`topDown`](#topDown) if you don't already have a tuple to convert from
(for example coming from [turboMaCk/non-empty-list-alias](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/turboMaCk/non-empty-list-alias/latest/List-NonEmpty#NonEmpty))
values.type: ( top, List.List belowElement ) -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElement) never_ Hand.Empty

values.name: indexLast
values.comment:
The position of the element at the bottom.

    import Stack exposing (onTopLay)

    Stack.only 3
        |> onTopLay 2
        |> Stack.indexLast
    --> 1
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top_ belowElement_) Basics.Never Hand.Empty -> Basics.Int

values.name: length
values.comment:
How many element there are.

    import Stack exposing (onTopLay)

    Stack.only 3
        |> onTopLay 2
        |> Stack.length
    --> 2
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top_ belowElement_) possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty -> Basics.Int

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every element based on its current value and `{ index }`.

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> Stack.map (\_ -> negate)
    --> topDown -1 [ -4, -9 ]

    topDown 1 [ 2, 3, 4 ]
        |> Stack.map (\{ index } n -> index * n)
    --> topDown 0 [ 2, 6, 12 ]
values.type: ({ index : Basics.Int } -> element -> elementMapped) -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked elementMapped) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: onTopGlue
values.comment:
Put the elements of a `List` on [`top`](#top).

    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopStack)

    topDown 1 [ 2 ]
        |> Stack.onTopGlue [ -1, 0 ]
    --> topDown -1 [ 0, 1, 2 ]

`onTopGlue` only when the piped stack food is already `... Never Empty`.
Glue a stack on top with

  - [`onTopStack`](#onTopStack) takes on the `possiblyOrNever Empty` type of the piped food
  - [`onTopStackAdapt`](#onTopStackAdapt) takes on the **`possiblyOrNever Empty` type of the argument**
values.type: List.List element -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: onTopLay
values.comment:
Add an element to the front.

    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopLay)

    topDown 2 [ 3 ] |> onTopLay 1
    --> topDown 1 [ 2, 3 ]

    Hand.empty |> onTopLay 1
    --> Stack.only 1
values.type: newTop -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked belowElement) possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow newTop belowElement) never_ Hand.Empty

values.name: onTopStack
values.comment:
Glue the elements of a stack to the end of the stack.

    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopStack)

    topDown 1 [ 2 ]
        |> onTopStack (topDown -1 [ 0 ])
    --> topDown -1 [ 0, 1, 2 ]

  - [`onTopStack`](#onTopStack) takes on the `possiblyOrNever Empty` type of the piped food
  - [`onTopStackAdapt`](#onTopStackAdapt) takes on the `possiblyOrNever Empty` type of the argument
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNeverAppended_ Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: onTopStackAdapt
values.comment:
Glue the elements of a `... Empty possiblyOrNever`/`Never` [`Stack`](#Stacked) to the [`top`](#top) of this [`Stack`](Stack).

    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopStack, onTopStackAdapt)

    Hand.empty
        |> onTopStackAdapt (topDown 1 [ 2 ])
        |> onTopStack (topDown -2 [ -1, 0 ])
    --> topDown -2 [ -1, 0, 1, 2 ]

  - [`onTopStack`](#onTopStack) takes on the `possiblyOrNever Empty` type of the piped food
  - [`onTopStackAdapt`](#onTopStackAdapt) takes on the `possiblyOrNever Empty` type of the argument
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNeverIn_ Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: only
values.comment:
A stack with just 1 single element.

    import Hand
    import Stack exposing (onTopLay)

    Stack.only ":)"
    --> Hand.empty |> onTopLay ":)"
values.type: top -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowTopElement_) never_ Hand.Empty

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Flip the order of the elements.

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown "l" [ "i", "v", "e" ]
        |> Stack.reverse
    --> topDown "e" [ "v", "i", "l" ]
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert it to a `List`.

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown 1 [ 7 ] |> Stack.toList
    --> [ 1, 7 ]

Don't try to use this prematurely.
Keeping type information as long as possible is always a win.
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked element) possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty -> List.List element

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Convert it to a `String`.

    import Stack exposing (topDown)

    topDown 'H' [ 'i' ] |> Stack.toString
    --> "Hi"

Don't try to use this prematurely.
Keeping type information as long as possible is always a win.
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked Char.Char) possiblyOrNever_ Hand.Empty -> String.String

values.name: toTopDown
values.comment:
Convert to a non-empty list tuple `( top, List belowElement )`.

Currently equivalent to [`fill`](Hand#fill).

    import Stack exposing (topDown, toTopDown)

    topDown "hi" [ "there", "👋" ]
        |> toTopDown
    --> ( "hi", [ "there", "👋" ] )
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElement) Basics.Never Hand.Empty -> ( top, List.List belowElement )

values.name: top
values.comment:
The first value.

    import Stack exposing (top, onTopLay)

    Stack.only 3
        |> onTopLay 2
        |> top
    --> 2
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowTop_) Basics.Never Hand.Empty -> top

values.name: topDown
values.comment: A stack from a top element and a `List` of elements below going down.
values.type: top -> List.List belowElement -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElement) never_ Hand.Empty

values.name: topMap
values.comment:
Change the [`top`](#top) element based on its current value.
Its type is allowed to change.

    import Stack exposing (topDown, topMap)

    topDown 1 [ 4, 9 ]
        |> topMap negate
    --> topDown -1 [ 4, 9 ]
values.type: (top -> topMapped) -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top belowElement) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow topMapped belowElement) possiblyOrNever Hand.Empty

values.name: topRemove
values.comment:
Everything after the first value.

    import Stack exposing (topDown, onTopLay, onTopStack, topRemove)

    Stack.only 2
        |> onTopLay 3
        |> onTopStack (topDown 1 [ 0 ])
        |> topRemove
    --> topDown 0 [ 3, 2 ]
    --: Hand (Stacked number_) Possibly Empty
values.type: Hand.Hand (Stack.StackTopBelow top_ belowElement) Basics.Never Hand.Empty -> Hand.Hand (Stack.Stacked belowElement) Possibly.Possibly Hand.Empty
